business.type_label: bespoke bridal salon (custom wedding gown design studio)

engine.ladder_reasoning: Site sells a defined bespoke design service (nearly four decades of expertise, a named 4-step process, commitment statement, 7 testimonials) — the visitor is deciding to trust a firm/craftspeople, not just browsing gowns. Portfolio and salon details are supporting proof, not the spine.

goal.one_action: Book an in-store private bridal design consultation

structure.archetype_note: Classic multi-page trust brochure funneling every page to a single Calendly consultation booking on /contact; a secondary Squarespace Shop/Cart exists but is not the primary landing conversion.

mapped.observed_as: top nav (HOME / THE PROCESS / BESPOKE BRIDES / COMMON QUESTIONS / CONTACT / Shop)
the bespoke process / The Bespoke Process (4-step timeline)
YOUR WEDDING EXPERIENCE / Our Commitment / FIT. FABRIC. EXPERIENCE.
PRIVATE CONSULTATIONS / Your dream gown is waiting to be DESIGNED / BOOK YOURS TODAY
BESPOKE BRIDES (portfolio of 7 named bespoke gowns)
COMMON QUESTIONS (Boutique Services / Fabrication / Pricing / Timeline accordions)

confidence.why: fetch_quality=rendered (not full) so capped at medium; Calendly booking is an opaque iframe (no field list obtainable) and the Squarespace Shop/Cart depth was not captured.

gap_findings: Dual model: a trust/booking brochure with a co-resident Squarespace Shop/Cart checkout — taxonomy forces a single engine + single conversion action, but this site legitimately has two (book consult AND buy). Not fully expressible.
Pricing surfaces only inside the FAQ accordion (~$5000 starting), not a real pricing section — 'pricing' canonical maps awkwardly to a single FAQ row.
